authoritative_sources.title: The Rule on Unfair or Deceptive Fees: Frequently Asked Questions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection authority for unfair or deceptive commerce practices. This official compliance guide is authoritative for total-price disclosure, live-event ticket fees, third-party ticketing, secondary ticketing, and fee-related misrepresentation boundaries.
used_for: live-event ticket price and fee transparency

authoritative_sources.title: ADA Requirements: Ticket Sales
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOJ is the authoritative federal enforcer of ADA Title III public-accommodation requirements. This ticket-sales guidance is authoritative for accessible seating inventory, comparable pricing, sales-channel parity, hold-release rules, and misuse-prevention boundaries at arts and entertainment venues.
used_for: limits on unsafe conclusions about accessible ticket availability

authoritative_sources.title: Visitor Use Statistics FAQ
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The National Park Service publishes official visitor-use definitions, count procedures, and refresh cadence for a major U.S. recreation system. It is authoritative public guidance for the meaning of visits, recreation visits, overnight stays, and official visitor-counting discipline.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST Handbook 130 - Current Edition
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST Office of Weights and Measures publishes the public baseline used by many U.S. jurisdictions for price verification procedures, making it authoritative for reconciling posted price, ticket price, parking charge, concession charge, and promotion evidence.

authoritative_sources.title: Better Online Ticket Sales Act
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the federal enforcer for the Better Online Ticket Sales Act. The statute is authoritative for automated ticket-buying circumvention, access-control abuse, and resale of improperly obtained tickets.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Customer exception handling with policy-aware escalation
used_for: duplicate or suspicious ticket acquisition handling

authoritative_sources.title: Protecting Personal Information: A Guide for Business
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC business guidance is an authoritative public reference for protecting customer information, limiting unnecessary retention, and handling sensitive payment and identity data in a way that prevents unsafe analytical disclosure.
used_for: limits on exposing payment or personal data in reports

authoritative_sources.title: Fighting Identity Theft with the Red Flags Rule: A How-To Guide for Business
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC business guidance is the authoritative public baseline for recognizing identity-theft red flags and deciding when ticketing, membership, account, or tender anomalies must route to fraud or loss-prevention owners.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Customer exception handling with policy-aware escalation

authoritative_sources.title: PCI Data Security Standard (PCI DSS)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCI SSC is the recognized cross-brand standards body for payment-card security. PCI DSS is authoritative for merchants and venues that store, process, or transmit cardholder data, constraining payment-record handling and analytics evidence usage.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Escalation for stale, conflicting, or incomplete data

authoritative_sources.title: Venue Guide for Security Enhancements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CISA is the federal risk-management agency for commercial facilities. Its venue guide is authoritative public guidance for threat mitigation, physical-security planning, and the boundary between analytic observations and operational security decisions at venues and attractions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Customer exception handling with policy-aware escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Management's Discussion and Analysis, Selected Financial Data, and Supplementary Financial Information
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SEC KPI guidance is the authoritative public baseline for how operational metrics must be defined, contextualized, caveated, and updated when visitor analytics are used in executive, board, investor, or other formal disclosure settings.

workflow_stages.name: Source collection and validation
workflow_stages.description: Collect ticketing, booking, access-control, POS, CRM, service-desk, refund, payment, and incident records and determine whether the evidence set is current and sufficient for the visitor-analytics request.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Metric calculation or report assembly
workflow_stages.description: Resolve visitor metric logic, denominators, count procedures, period cutoffs, occupancy assumptions, and source precedence before dashboard or analytical package assembly begins.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Narrative synthesis or disclosure review
workflow_stages.description: Assemble visitor-analysis language, caveats, thresholds, and confidence notes while ensuring audience-facing outputs do not imply unsupported certainty, causality, or signoff.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Escalation for stale, conflicting, or incomplete data
workflow_stages.description: Preserve unresolved source conflicts, stale feeds, missing count procedures, and unsupported claims rather than publishing through them.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: High-volume intake or service execution
workflow_stages.description: Keep analysis grounded in sellouts, holiday weekends, festival surges, timed-entry congestion, and attraction spikes without turning the lane into frontline command authority.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Customer exception handling with policy-aware escalation
workflow_stages.description: Route refund exceptions, pricing overrides, suspicious ticketing, crowd-safety indicators, and security-sensitive incidents to the correct adjacent specialist with complete evidence packaging.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

artifacts: ticket orders, reservations, timed-entry bookings, waitlist states, and visitor or customer records
access-control scans, turnstile counts, attendance logs, manual count procedures, and occupancy tallies
POS transactions, payment records, refund records, chargebacks, comps, promotions, parking records, and ancillary-spend extracts
membership and season-pass records, CRM notes, service-desk tickets, and complaint histories
queue metrics, dwell-time estimates, attraction throughput records, and event or exhibit calendars
incident notes, bag-check exceptions, security logs, suspicious-scan records, and loss-prevention artifacts
metric dictionaries, reporting calendars, disclosure templates, signoff checklists, and approval matrices
systems_of_record: venue management or event operations platform
general ledger and finance reporting systems

allowed: Define visitor metrics and source hierarchies when the venue context, audience, and reporting purpose are explicit.
Assemble evidence-backed analytical briefs, caveats, reconciliation notes, and disclosure-risk summaries.
Separate tickets sold, scanned attendance, visits, unique visitors, occupancy, and spend metrics when the evidence requires different definitions.
escalate: Escalate when pricing changes, ticket-release decisions, refund exceptions, appeasement authority, or comp policy determines the answer.
Escalate when suspicious scans, chargebacks, account misuse, or resale abuse require fraud or loss-prevention investigation.
Escalate when crowd-safety, incident response, accessibility remediation, or formal disclosure approval is required.
refuse: Refuse unsupported causal, benchmark, or no-safety-exposure claims not supported by named records and approved methodology.
Refuse to approve pricing, refund, inventory-release, fraud, security, or disclosure outcomes.
Refuse to publish or imply certainty when source quality, signoff, or metric definitions remain unresolved.

domain_failure_modes: tickets sold are treated as attendance without scan or count logic
gate scans double-count re-entry or staff access and inflate visits
timed-entry capacity, comp-ticket, or accessibility rules are ignored when comparing utilization
stale access-control or POS feeds are treated as final even though preliminary data can change
parking, concessions, or merchandise spend is blended into per-cap metrics without denominator alignment
weather closures, security incidents, or venue holds are omitted from visitor narratives
duplicate scans, chargebacks, or suspicious ticket acquisition are handled as routine analytics instead of fraud-sensitive cases
guest appeasement, pricing, or safety decisions are implied from analytics outputs without authority

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Metric definition is ambiguous or disputed
Source data is incomplete, stale, or conflicting
Stakeholder asks for a claim or disclosure not supported by the data
Different systems disagree on the metric outcome
A stakeholder asks for stronger conclusions than the data supports
Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications

unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Public baseline uses explicit differentiation between visits, attendance scans, and unique visitors. Tenant metric dictionaries and source-precedence rules must map local labels to this baseline instead of silently replacing it.

notes: The source set covers workflow, evidence, terminology, failure handling, and disclosure posture for ticketed attractions, venues, museums, festivals, and recreation settings without collapsing back into generic BI.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated package because venue-specific metric variation is handled through explicit tenant adaptation rather than guessing.
